title: rosa canina subvar. bihariensis
intro: Rosa canina subvar. bihariensis, also known as the Bihari rose or Indian rose, is a subspecies of the wild rose plant (Rosa canina) native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rosa canina subvar. bihariensis include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and reduce symptoms of colds and flu. Rosa canina subvar. bihariensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
